headline: Chevron’s Venezuela Opening And Hormuz Shock Reframe Valuation Debate
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) is facing a reshaped risk profile as a U.S.-led move removes Venezuela’s president, clearing the way for higher production from its Venezuelan operations. At the same time, Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is disrupting global oil trade routes and pushing energy prices higher. These events are increasing the importance of Chevron’s assets outside the Middle East and its role as a key U.S. operator in Venezuela. Chevron sits at the center of global oil and gas, with...
